name: EPS Type I (1.0 pcf)
note: Bead-board EPS; vapor-open; lowest cost; not for high-traffic areas. R/in stable over time (no blowing-agent loss).

name: EPS Type II (1.5 pcf)
note: Common below-grade and CI choice. R-value stable.

name: EPS Type IX (2.0 pcf)
note: Higher density EPS for roof and below slab.

name: XPS Type X (15 psi)
note: Aged R per LTTR; HFC blowing agents being phased out (HFO replacement). Foam manufacturers transitioning 2021–2025.

name: XPS Type IV (25 psi)
note: Below-grade workhorse; closed-cell; resists water absorption.

name: Polyiso (foil-faced) — aged LTTR
note: Aged value per CAN/ULC S770 LTTR. Foil facer makes vapor impermeable. R drops at sub-40 °F service temps in cold climates (be conservative for Boston).

name: Polyiso (glass-faced)
note: Glass-mat facer is more vapor-permeable than foil. Aged R drops more in cold service.

name: Mineral wool board (semi-rigid)
note: Vapor-open; non-combustible (Class A); does not lose R over time. Common products: Rockwool Comfortboard 80/110.

name: Wood-fiber insulation board
note: Vapor-open; renewable. Products: Gutex/Steico/TimberHP. Class B/C flame.

name: Closed-cell spray polyurethane foam (2 lb/ft³)
typical_use: Cavity fill & rim joist; below grade interior
note: Aged R; functions as vapor retarder Class II at 2"; structural strengthening of stud walls; thermal-barrier code requires 15-min coverage if exposed.

name: Open-cell spray polyurethane foam (~0.5 lb/ft³)
note: Vapor-open. Not for below grade. Class III vapor retarder. Thermal-barrier code requires 15-min coverage if exposed.

name: Phenolic foam board
note: Highest R per inch; limited US availability; can be acidic — historical issues with corrosion of steel fasteners. Verify product.
